title: Require Service Contract Authority
forbids: a module contract without one generic exported contract anchor or canonical TypeBox adaptation at every input/output; private support that is not bounded and reachable when present; errors that are not private local Effect-oRPC constructors when present; nonlocal or dynamic error-map authority; and any exported parallel schema, type, envelope, error-map, or helper authority
why: A module contract is one exported authority. Bounded private support can reduce repeated syntax without turning schemas, errors, envelopes, or helpers into competing public boundaries.
remediate: When contract-local schemas, error maps, shared fragments, or helpers are present, keep them private and syntactically reachable from the single exported contract. Apply schema(TypeBoxSchema) at every input/output. When public errors are present, define private Effect-oRPC constructors locally and attach them through inline or bounded local error maps. Remove imported, dynamic, or exported parallel authority.
message: Service module contracts must preserve one declarative schema and public-error authority.
